title: Intel Ends Work On Quantum Compiler Open-Source Bits - Phoronix
why:
- Intel has formally discontinued its Quantum Passes open-source project, which provided additional compiler passes for the LLVM-based Intel Quantum SDK compiler.
- The Intel Quantum SDK is described as a "complete quantum computing stack" for code development and simulation, intended to prepare developers for future quantum hardware.
- The Quantum Passes project enabled developers to add custom compiler passes during the compilation process and helped prepare code before it was translated into quantum instructions for Intel Quantum Backends.
- The Quantum Passes repository is now archived as read-only, and Intel will no longer provide development, support, maintenance, or accept patches for the project.
- The project had been inactive for at least a year prior to its discontinuation, coinciding with a lack of updates to the main Intel Quantum SDK.
